title: Fgentic partner trust registry
description: Single validated source of truth for cross-organization federation trust (issue #349). Every enforcement plane is derived from this document; check:fed-registry validates against this schema and fails closed on any plane that drifts from it.

server_name.description: Exact Matrix server_name (FQDN, never a localpart — D6). Lowercase DNS name with an optional :port.

role.description: host = the local exporting homeserver; admitted = an authorized remote partner; denied = the deny-by-default control server (present in no enforcement plane).

contained.description: Break-glass emergency containment (issue #350). When true, the partner is dropped from the callback border (policy.json allowed_servers) regardless of allowlist membership, so every federated event from it is denied. Set by 'fed:break-glass', cleared by 'fed:restore'. GitOps-reversible.

classification.description: Highest data classification this partner may exchange. A denied partner is 'none'.

review_by.description: Time-bounded trust (issue #463): UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD) by which this partner's controls must be re-reviewed. Required for an admitted partner. A passed review_by raises the review-due alert but does not fail the gate; renew by re-signing the agreement with a new window.

valid_until.description: Time-bounded trust (issue #463): optional UTC hard-expiry date (YYYY-MM-DD). A passed valid_until FAILS check:fed-registry closed, blocking reconciliation of federation trust config until the partner is renewed or offboarded. It is not a live traffic kill-switch — immediate containment is fed:break-glass (#350).

card_additional_key_ids.description: Zero-downtime AgentCard signing-key rotation overlap window (#352, Task 4). Optional set of ADDITIONAL currently-valid signing key IDs; a card verifies under card_key_id OR any of these (the primary stays authoritative). Each kid is unique, non-empty, distinct from card_key_id, and MUST NOT appear in card_revoked_key_ids — revocation wins, and a pinned-and-revoked kid fails check:fed-registry closed. Maps to the bridge cardIdentity.additionalKeys[].keyID (#920); the signer lifecycle serves the matching public JWKs. Absent = today's single-key behavior.

card_revoked_key_ids.description: AgentCard signing-key revocation signal (#352, Task 4). Optional set of RETIRED signing key IDs; a card offered under one is refused deterministically (fail-closed) even if cryptographically valid. Each kid is unique and non-empty; a revoked kid must not also be pinned as card_key_id or in card_additional_key_ids. Maps to the bridge cardIdentity.revokedKeyIDs (#920). Absent = nothing revoked.
